description: Import a grid from a file. The file named s is imported. A path can be part of the file name. If no extension is specified, .f3grid is assumed in FLAC3D and .f2grid is assumed in FLAC2D .

6.0.description: Import a grid from a file. The file named s is imported. A path can be part of the file name. If no extension is specified,.f3grid is assumed.

keywords.name: use-given-ids
keywords.description: Forces FLAC3D to use the same gridpoint and zone IDs as are specified in the import file.

keywords.name: format
keywords.description: Specified the expected format of the input file, overriding any default decision made by checking the file extension. The following format types are available:

keywords.name: format-flac3d
keywords.description: This specifies the native FLAC3D ASCII- or binary-format grid file, with an expected extension of f3grid. The binary-format grid file is usually with smaller size and imported quicker than the ASCII-format grid file. The details of ASCII-format of FLAC3D grid file is given below.

keywords.name: format-flac3d-ansys
keywords.description: This specifies an ANSYS compatible mesh file, with a normally expected extension of lis. Note that the ANSYS format enabled here generally requires two different files, both with the same extension. The first file is assumed to contain the gridpoint information, and the second the zone information. Use “nlist” command in ANSYS to output nodes (gridpoints) to a file; and use “elist” comamnd in ANSYS to output elements (zones) into another file. Any ANSYS element with more than 8 nodes is not supported.

keywords.name: format-flac3d-abaqus
keywords.description: This specifies an ABAQUS compatible mesh file, with an expected extension of inp. Acceptable ABAQUS elements are “c3d4”, “c3d5”, “c3d6”, and “c3d8”. Other ABAQUS elements are not supported.

keywords.name: format-flac3d

The binary-format grid file is usually with smaller size and imported quicker than the ASCII-format grid file. The details of ASCII-format of FLAC3D grid file is given below.

Note that the ANSYS format enabled here generally requires two different files, both with the same extension. The first file is assumed to contain the gridpoint information, and the second the zone information. Use “nlist” command in ANSYS to output nodes (gridpoints) to a file; and use “elist” comamnd in ANSYS to output elements (zones) into another file. Any ANSYS element with more than 8 nodes is not supported.

keywords.name: use-given-ids
keywords.description: Force FLAC to use the same gridpoint and zone IDs as are specified in the import file.

keywords.name: format-midas
keywords.description: Specify the expected format of the input file, overriding any default decision made by checking the file extension. The following format types are available. Specify the native FLAC3D ASCII- or binary-format grid file, with an expected extension of f3grid . The binary-format grid file is usually with smaller size and imported quicker than the ASCII-format grid file. The details of ASCII-format of FLAC grid file is given below. Specify ANSYS compatible mesh files, with an expected extension of lis . Note that the ANSYS format enabled here generally requires two different files, both with the same extension. The first file is assumed to contain the node/gridpoint information, and the second the element/zone information. Use “nlist” command in ANSYS to output nodes (gridpoints) to a file; and use “elist” command in ANSYS to output elements (zones) into another file. Any ANSYS element with more than 8 nodes is not supported. Specify an ABAQUS compatible mesh file, with an expected extension of inp . Acceptable ABAQUS elements are “c3d4”, “c3d5”, “c3d6”, and “c3d8”. Other ABAQUS elements are not supported. Specify MIDAS compatible mesh files, which should be exported from MIDAS into Excel files with an expected extension of csv . Note that the MIDAS format enabled here generally requires two different files, both with the same extension. The first file is assumed to contain the node/gridpoint information, and the second the element/zone information. Only 1-order element types, e.g., tetrahedron, pentahedron(pyramid), hexahedron) are supported. Each line of the nodes information file should starts with node ID and ends with three components of node coordinates. Each line of the element information file should starts with element ID, element type (tetrahedron, pentahedron, or hexahedron), and group name/ID, and ends with node ID list.
